message: Suggested next: T100189 — Define lease UX status contract and concise blocked responses

readyQueue.title: Define lease UX status contract and concise blocked responses
readyQueue.approach: Narrow Phase 93 to the foundation that downstream UX work needs: expose a stable lease status vocabulary and make lease-denied command responses compact, truthful, and actionable. Follow-up tickets in the same phase cover extension controls, bounded wait behavior, and suspect drift detection.
technicalScope: Extend lease status/classification helpers so callers with an agentSessionId can distinguish lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, and stale/invalid lease states; reserve suspect/drift semantics for the suspect-drift follow-up ticket.
Revise workspace-edit-lease-held and related non-owner responses to include holder session id, task id when present, expiry, available alternatives, and one recommended next action.
Keep blocked responses compact for agents and avoid suggestions that require broad instruction/catalog reloads or expensive polling.
Document the response/status vocabulary consumed by the extension and follow-up same-phase tickets.
Add focused tests for status classification and denied-response payloads.
acceptanceCriteria: Denied claim, heartbeat, and release paths for active leases held by another session return holder identity, task id when present, expiry, alternatives, and one recommended next action in stable JSON.
A caller-provided agentSessionId can classify lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, and stale/invalid states without opening task SQLite or mutating the lease.
Agent-facing blocked copy is concise and does not tell agents to reload broad instructions or poll aggressively.
The shared vocabulary is documented in the relevant command instruction or contract surface for extension consumers.
Automated tests cover same-holder, other-holder, missing, stale/invalid, and compact blocked-response cases.
readyQueue.summary: Define the shared lease status contract and low-token blocked-response behavior that Phase 93 UX work builds on.
readyQueue.description: Define the shared lease status contract and low-token blocked-response behavior that Phase 93 UX work builds on.
readyQueue.risk: Medium: this changes command response contracts that agents and the extension will consume; keep the schema additive and deterministic.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Make the safe path feel like guidance instead of a mysterious failure.
Reduce wasted AI credits when an agent cannot edit yet.
Help users recover from stale or suspect lease states without manual file surgery.
blockers: T100188 is completed and provides clone-local lease commands under git common-dir.
Extension work still needs a stable session identifier where available; fallback behavior must be explicit when no session id exists.
The exact edit-capable flows that should honor waitForLease are not enumerated yet; this phase must define the first supported integration points before wiring broad enforcement.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

Current code exposes claim/heartbeat/release/status commands, workspace-coordination-status, a status-bar posture, and a read-only Coordination status card. It does not yet expose held-by-me vs held-by-other status, suspect drift status, extension lease actions, or waitForLease behavior.

ticketReview.verdict: Direction is truthful, but the original ticket was too broad for one implementation task and implied several states/behaviors that are not present yet.
evidence: T100188 is completed with lease command delivery evidence.
src/modules/task-engine/workspace-edit-lease-commands-runtime.ts has claim, heartbeat, release, and workspace-edit-status command behavior.

src/modules/task-engine/coordination/build-workspace-coordination-status.ts emits read-only posture and lease active/stale information, but not holder identity or drift comparison against the lease fingerprint.
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/extension.ts renders only a compact coordination posture in the status bar.
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/views/status/render-status-tab.ts renders a read-only Coordination card with lease active/stale/none only.
missingInformation: Which VS Code/session identifier should be treated as the durable agentSessionId for held-by-me vs held-by-other classification.
Which commands or edit-capable flows should accept waitForLease in the first implementation slice.
Whether recover stale and claim/release actions should require explicit per-click policy approval copy in the extension or reuse existing command-client approval handling.
How suspect state should be prioritized relative to dirty_workspace, dirty_task_db, and lease_held posture when multiple signals are true.

readyQueue.title: Define documentation lifecycle taxonomy
readyQueue.approach: Establish documentation lifecycle taxonomy. Planning note: execution phaseKeys jump 97→106 because 98–105 are intentionally unused in this roadmap; coordination work occupies 92–97 beforehand.
technicalScope: Planning phaseKey gap: coordination program uses 92–97; documentation program resumes at 106 (98–105 reserved/unused by design).
Add or update the maintainer-facing documentation lifecycle policy in the appropriate docs location.
Define required metadata for audience, owner, canonical source, generated status, replacement path, and deletion condition.
Link the policy from human and agent documentation entrypoints without changing source-of-truth precedence.
acceptanceCriteria: The repository has a documented taxonomy for active, generated, historical, archive-candidate, and delete-candidate docs.
The policy clearly states where agent-first docs, human docs, generated docs, planning docs, and archives belong.
AGENTS or maintainer index surfaces point contributors to the lifecycle policy.
readyQueue.summary: Define the documentation lifecycle taxonomy that separates canonical agent guidance, canonical human docs, generated human docs, data sources, historical material, archive candidates, and delete candidates.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan
metadata.planningPhaseKeyNote: phaseKeys 98–105 intentionally unused between coordination phase 97 and documentation phase 106.

readyQueue.title: Delivery Evidence Harvester command
readyQueue.approach: Implement workspace-kit run command that inspects current git branch, linked GitHub PR metadata when configured, CI or check conclusions when available, merge SHA when merged, and recorded local validation commands; emit deterministic JSON preview and optional apply that writes task.metadata.deliveryEvidence per the v1 schema referenced by the delivery-evidence guard.
technicalScope: Reuse or extend phase-delivery-preflight and delivery-evidence validation helpers where practical.
Non-interactive CLI with deterministic stdout, structured error codes, and remediation hints listing missing fields and follow-up commands.
Preview vs apply semantics aligned with other task-engine mutators, including expectedPlanningGeneration when persisting task rows.
Optional GitHub network reads behind existing auth and config; explicit failure modes when credentials or context are missing.
Automated tests for preview-only success, missing PR, missing checks, and metadata write path.
acceptanceCriteria: Against a typical task branch with an open PR, preview output is valid JSON and matches deliveryEvidence schemaVersion 1 where signals exist.
When signals are missing, response names each absent field and cites concrete next commands or config keys.
Apply path can populate metadata.deliveryEvidence on a target task id without SQLite or hand-edited JSON store edits.
pnpm run check passes for touched packages.
run-transition complete behavior unchanged for advisory and off deliveryEvidence enforcement modes.

readyQueue.title: Enforce task-state authority on control branches
readyQueue.approach: Add explicit task-state authority configuration and resolver. Mutating task-engine commands should proceed on authority branches and produce intents or structured denials on worker branches, while reads remain available everywhere.
technicalScope: Add tasks.stateAuthority config with mode, branch patterns, and workerBranchMutations behavior.
Implement authority resolver returning authority, worker, unknown, or disabled with explain entries.
Embed compact authority posture in dashboard-summary, agent-bootstrap, and workspace coordination status.
Gate task mutating commands including run-transition, create-task, update-task, apply-task-batch, wishlist conversions, phase assignment, and dependency mutations.
Add doctor or delivery-loop warnings when .workspace-kit/tasks/workspace-kit.db is modified on a worker branch.
acceptanceCriteria: Task mutations on configured authority branches preserve existing expectedPlanningGeneration behavior.
Task mutations on worker branches are blocked or converted to intents according to config.
Read-only task commands continue to work from worker branches.
Dirty task DB on a worker branch is reported by doctor or coordination status with remediation guidance.
Tests cover authority, worker, detached/unknown, disabled config, and each major mutation category.
readyQueue.summary: Prevent feature branches from creating branch-local task DB timelines by routing or blocking task mutations.
readyQueue.description: Prevent feature branches from creating branch-local task DB timelines by routing or blocking task mutations.
readyQueue.risk: High: this touches task mutation routing and can interrupt established maintainer workflows if defaults are too strict.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Stop task-engine drift across branches.
Keep the current checked-in task state model usable while reducing binary DB merge risk.
Prepare a clean migration path toward a future state branch backend.
blockers: Requires Phase 93 extension lease UX/actions (T100212).
Requires Phase 93 bounded wait/read-only behavior (T100213).
Requires Phase 93 suspect lease drift detection (T100214).
Requires agreement on default authority branches for Workflow Cannon repositories.
Must avoid breaking existing single-branch local users; provide disabled or advisory modes.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

readyQueue.title: Add task mutation intents for worker branches
readyQueue.approach: Introduce a clone-local intent queue under git common-dir for task mutations requested outside the authority branch. The dashboard and authority checkout can list, apply, or reject intents using normal task-engine validation and planning-generation checks.
technicalScope: Define task mutation intent schema with intentId, kind, taskId, requestedAction, payload, evidence, createdBy, branch, headSha, worktreePath, createdAt, and advisory planningGeneration.
Implement create-task-mutation-intent, list-task-mutation-intents, apply-task-mutation-intent, and reject-task-mutation-intent commands.
Store v1 intents at .git/workflow-cannon/intents/*.json with atomic writes and validation.
Integrate worker-branch mutation gating so blocked mutations can emit an intent when configured.
Add dashboard queue for pending intents with apply/reject affordances and clear policy approval handling.
acceptanceCriteria: A worker branch run-transition request can create a complete/start/block intent without modifying task SQLite.
Applying an intent on an authority branch invokes the normal command path and respects planning-generation and policy approval gates.
Rejected intents are retained or archived with rationale rather than silently deleted.
Intent listing is compact and bounded for agent use.
Tests cover create, list, apply success, apply stale-generation conflict, reject, malformed intent, and duplicate intent id.
readyQueue.summary: Let agents on worker branches request task updates without mutating branch-local task state.
readyQueue.description: Let agents on worker branches request task updates without mutating branch-local task state.
readyQueue.risk: Medium-to-high: intent queues can become another backlog if not surfaced clearly and pruned deliberately.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Let agents keep momentum on worker branches without corrupting task state.
Give humans and authority agents a reviewable queue of requested task changes.
Create a stepping stone toward a dedicated state branch or event ledger.
blockers: Depends on Phase 94 mutation classification and authority resolver.
Needs policy approval UX for applying sensitive intents.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

readyQueue.title: Make checkpoints stash and branch guards lease-aware
readyQueue.approach: Thread workspace edit lease checks through auto-checkpoint, stash, rewind, and branch-helper surfaces. Prefer exact checkpoint refs or patch artifacts over anonymous stash positions, and mark leases suspect when external branch changes are detected.
technicalScope: Update auto-checkpoint to allow dirty checkpoint/stash only when no lease exists or the caller owns the active lease.
Fail closed when another session owns the lease and a command would stash, rewind, checkout, reset, or otherwise alter workspace files.
Record exact stash commit SHAs or checkpoint IDs and never rely on stash@{0} for recovery semantics.
Add patch/checkpoint artifact mode for dirty work where feasible, with stash as fallback only.
Add branch switch guard helpers and extension warnings for external branch/HEAD changes during active lease.
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-checkpoint refuses to stash when a different active lease holder exists.
Lease holder checkpoint behavior records exact recoverable refs and excludes task-state files according to existing safety rules.
Branch switch helpers refuse or require explicit owner override during an active lease.
External branch changes mark the lease suspect and appear in coordination status.
Tests cover no-lease, owner-lease, other-lease, stale-lease, exact stash SHA recording, and suspect branch change cases.
readyQueue.summary: Prevent Workflow Cannon safety tooling from stashing or switching underneath another active agent.
readyQueue.description: Prevent Workflow Cannon safety tooling from stashing or switching underneath another active agent.
readyQueue.risk: High: checkpoint and rewind paths are safety-critical; incorrect behavior can lose or misapply local work.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Remove the sharpest multi-agent stash and branch-switch footguns.
Keep recovery precise and auditable.
Make existing checkpoint safety compatible with edit leases.
blockers: Depends on Phase 92 lease core and Phase 93 suspect-state UX.
Patch artifact mode may need careful size and path limits to avoid storing vendor trees or secrets.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

readyQueue.title: Add extension lease status UI and actions
readyQueue.approach: Use the T100189 status vocabulary to make lease state visible and actionable in the VS Code extension without requiring users to inspect raw CLI JSON.
technicalScope: Update the status bar, Status tab Coordination card, or dashboard surface to show lease-free, held-by-me, held-by-other, stale/invalid, and suspect-ready display states using compact labels.
Show lease holder session, task id when present, expiry, and stale/invalid state when available from the command contract.
Add extension commands or QuickPick actions for claim, release, recover stale, inspect holder/status, and continue read-only/plan-only.
Pass the best available agentSessionId into lease commands and define a clear fallback when the extension cannot identify the current session.
Keep sensitive lease mutations on the existing policyApproval path and avoid manual lease-file editing guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: The extension UI distinguishes lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, stale/invalid, and suspect-ready states without raw JSON inspection.
Users can launch claim, release, recover stale, inspect holder/status, and continue read-only actions from VS Code UI affordances.
Lease actions call workspace-kit commands rather than editing files directly and include policyApproval where the command is sensitive.
When no stable session id is available, the UI explains the limitation through action availability or fallback copy rather than misclassifying ownership.
Extension tests or renderer tests cover the major lease rendering states and at least one action dispatch path.
readyQueue.summary: Render lease state in the extension and add user actions for common lease operations.
readyQueue.description: Render lease state in the extension and add user actions for common lease operations.
readyQueue.risk: Medium: unclear ownership copy could cause users to override leases too casually.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
metadata.blockerReason: Depends on T100189 status vocabulary and blocked-response contract.
goals: Make lease state understandable inside VS Code.
Provide safe recovery and read-only paths without manual file surgery.
implementationNotes: Prefer existing dashboard/status rendering patterns and command-client paths.

readyQueue.title: Add bounded waitForLease behavior
readyQueue.approach: Define and implement an optional bounded wait path for lease-aware flows so blocked agents can wait cheaply or fall back to read-only planning.
technicalScope: Define waitForLease arguments or helper options, including timeout, interval, and fallback behavior, for the first supported lease-aware entrypoints.
Ensure waiting uses low-frequency checks, bounded timeout, and no tight polling loop.
Return deterministic JSON when the lease becomes available, times out, is stale/recoverable, or remains held by another session.
Keep read-only/plan-only alternatives available when waiting is disabled or times out.
Document which commands or flows support waitForLease in this phase.
acceptanceCriteria: waitForLease is opt-in or conservatively defaulted and has a bounded maximum timeout.
Polling frequency is low and tested, with no busy loop or broad context reload guidance.
Timeout and success responses include the current holder, task id when present, expiry, and recommended next action.
At least one lease-aware command or helper path uses the behavior end to end.
Automated tests cover immediate availability, active-holder timeout, stale/recoverable lease, and disabled/fallback behavior.
readyQueue.summary: Add a conservative waitForLease path with low-frequency checks and explicit timeout behavior.
readyQueue.description: Add a conservative waitForLease path with low-frequency checks and explicit timeout behavior.
readyQueue.risk: Medium: waiting behavior can waste agent credits if defaults are too aggressive.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
metadata.blockerReason: Depends on T100189 response vocabulary for compact wait and timeout messages.
goals: Reduce wasted AI credits when edits are temporarily blocked.
Give agents a bounded alternative to immediate failure.
implementationNotes: Define supported entrypoints before wiring broad enforcement.

readyQueue.title: Detect suspect lease checkout drift
readyQueue.approach: Compare the active lease fingerprint to the current checkout and surface suspect status when branch, HEAD, worktree, or dirty manifest signals change unexpectedly during a lease.
technicalScope: Compare active lease branch, headSha, worktreePath, and dirtyManifest against the current checkout fingerprint in read-only status paths.
Add stable suspect flags for branch drift, HEAD drift, worktree drift, and dirty manifest drift.
Surface suspect status through workspace-edit-status and workspace-coordination-status without mutating or deleting the lease.
Define posture precedence when suspect signals coexist with dirty workspace, dirty task DB, stale lease, or active lease.
Expose suspect status for extension consumers without requiring raw lease-file parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Branch, HEAD, worktree path, and dirty manifest changes during an active lease are detected and returned as stable suspect flags.
workspace-edit-status and workspace-coordination-status surface suspect status without mutating the lease file.
Posture precedence is documented and deterministic when multiple coordination signals are present.
The extension-facing payload can render suspect state without reading the lease file directly.
Automated tests cover no drift, branch drift, HEAD drift, dirty manifest drift, stale lease, and malformed lease cases.
readyQueue.summary: Mark active leases suspect when checkout state drifts from the lease fingerprint.
readyQueue.description: Mark active leases suspect when checkout state drifts from the lease fingerprint.
readyQueue.risk: Medium: false positives could make valid work look unsafe; prefer explicit flags over destructive recovery.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
metadata.blockerReason: Depends on T100189 status vocabulary so suspect can be rendered consistently.
goals: Help users recognize stale or unsafe lease state before mutating files.
Prepare reliable signals for extension rendering and later task-state authority gates.
implementationNotes: Detection must be read-only; recovery remains an explicit command/action.

readyQueue.title: Add isolated proposal mode and state branch preparation
readyQueue.approach: Introduce proposal branches/worktrees as explicit artifacts surfaced through diffs and PR actions. In parallel, add deterministic task-state export artifacts so a future workflow-cannon/state branch can use mergeable event/snapshot data while SQLite remains a runtime cache.
technicalScope: Add isolated proposal creation for a task with branch/worktree metadata, base branch, changed-file tracking, and discard/recover actions.
Surface proposals as View Diff, Apply, Open PR, and Discard rather than silently changing the visible workspace.
Bind proposal metadata to task ids and capture validation evidence plus any task mutation intents generated during proposal work.
Add deterministic task-state export of sorted snapshot JSON and append-only event JSONL from current SQLite state.
Prototype internal workflow-cannon/state backend design using event/snapshot artifacts as merge surface and SQLite as generated cache.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can create an isolated proposal for parallel work without changing the visible workspace edit lease holder.
Proposal changes are visible as bounded diffs and can be applied, opened as PR, or discarded explicitly.
Proposal task metadata and generated task intents are recoverable after restart.
Task-state export is deterministic across repeated runs from the same DB state.
A design note or ADR documents the future state branch backend, event replay model, and SQLite cache boundary.
readyQueue.summary: Support explicit parallel implementation proposals and prepare a mergeable task-state backend without making invisible edits the default.
readyQueue.description: Support explicit parallel implementation proposals and prepare a mergeable task-state backend without making invisible edits the default.
readyQueue.risk: Medium: proposal worktrees can create lifecycle clutter and token overhead if not bounded and surfaced cleanly.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Enable true parallel implementation without surprising invisible edits.
Prepare for a global task-state branch only after leases and authority rules are proven.
Move toward mergeable task-state artifacts while preserving current SQLite runtime performance.
blockers: Depends on stable lease, authority, and intent flows from prior phases.
State export must avoid leaking local-only policy traces or secrets.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

readyQueue.title: Inventory and classify documentation surfaces
technicalScope: Inventory root Markdown, .ai docs, docs/maintainers, module docs, task prompts, examples, exercises, and documentation module templates/views.
Classify each active documentation surface using the lifecycle taxonomy from the previous phase.
Record replacement paths, canonical sources, and delete/archive recommendations for stale planning and historical files.
acceptanceCriteria: A machine-readable or reviewable documentation ledger exists and covers all non-vendored Markdown documentation groups.
Root planning/status files and maintainer top-level docs have explicit keep, move, archive, or delete-candidate recommendations.
Generated and canonical documentation surfaces are distinguishable from hand-maintained prose.
readyQueue.summary: Create a repository documentation inventory that classifies Markdown and structured doc surfaces by audience, ownership, generated status, lifecycle status, and recommended disposition.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

readyQueue.title: Clean repo-root documentation surface
technicalScope: Review root Markdown files such as PLAN, INIT_PLAN, NEXT, CAE plans, phase release transcripts, MODULE_REFACTOR, and REVIEW against the inventory ledger.
Move surviving historical material to an archive path or fold still-current guidance into canonical .ai, docs/maintainers, module docs, or task-engine state.
Remove pointer-only stubs and obsolete root docs after confirming inbound links and generated surfaces are updated.
acceptanceCriteria: Repo-root Markdown is limited to intentional entrypoints such as README, AGENTS, CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG pointer, and license-adjacent files.
Historical root docs are either archived under the agreed docs archive structure or deleted with replacement evidence in the ledger.
No active agent or maintainer instruction points to deleted root planning files.
readyQueue.summary: Reduce repo-root documentation to the durable visitor and agent entrypoints, moving or deleting historical planning and phase transcript files according to the classification ledger.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

readyQueue.title: Normalize maintainer documentation structure
technicalScope: Update docs/maintainers navigation so humans can quickly find maintainer workflows, generated docs, plans, ADRs, archive material, and data artifacts.
Move or relink top-level maintainer docs into durable subfolders where that reduces clutter without breaking canonical generation rules.
Preserve generated-output banners and update coverage or exception manifests when paths move.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/maintainers/README.md acts as the human table of contents and explains generated vs hand-maintained areas.
Top-level docs/maintainers files are limited to deliberate front-door documents or compatibility pointers.
Moved docs retain working links and do not violate .ai to docs drift checks.
readyQueue.summary: Reshape docs/maintainers into an obvious human documentation library with clear folders for operations, releases, architecture, generated outputs, plans, archives, data, ADRs, runbooks, playbooks, and workbooks.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

readyQueue.title: Tighten agent documentation discoverability
technicalScope: Audit .ai subtrees, HUB/README entrypoints, machine CLI policy docs, playbooks, runbooks, workbooks, and module instruction files for navigation gaps.
Add or update compact agent-facing indexes where needed, using status and audience markers without expanding human prose into agent bootstrap paths.
Ensure human docs link to agent sources when useful, while routine agent procedure remains anchored in .ai, module instructions, and CLI JSON output.
acceptanceCriteria: Every active .ai subtree has a clear machine-oriented entrypoint or is intentionally covered by an existing routing file.
Agent-facing docs identify active, generated, historical, and deprecated material clearly enough for agents to avoid stale guidance.
No routine agent workflow requires reading long docs/maintainers prose when a canonical .ai or module instruction source exists.
readyQueue.summary: Make agent-optimized documentation easier for agents to discover while keeping it out of the way of human users and preserving the existing source-of-truth order.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

readyQueue.title: Enhance documentation module for classification and stale detection
technicalScope: Design and implement documentation-module support for classify-docs or equivalent inventory output.
Add lifecycle metadata support for audience, owner, canonical source, generated-by, last-reviewed, replacement, and deletion condition where appropriate.
Add stale-doc detection for root Markdown allowlist violations, old phase/version claims, generated-output edits, and unmapped .ai or docs surfaces.
Clarify or unify the boundary between the documentation module generation pipeline and the .ai to docs maintainer mirror pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: A documentation-module command or check can emit a useful documentation classification/staleness report.
Generated docs, data-source docs, canonical agent docs, and human-maintainer docs are represented consistently in module rules or manifests.
The implementation includes tests or checks that cover the new documentation lifecycle behavior.
readyQueue.summary: Extend the documentation module so documentation organization, lifecycle classification, stale-doc detection, and generated/human parity can be checked mechanically.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

readyQueue.title: Archive or delete stale documentation with evidence
readyQueue.phase: Phase 112 - Documentation archive and deletion pass
technicalScope: Create or update a deletion/archive register listing path, replacement, inbound links, task/release references, package impact, confidence, and final disposition.
Archive medium-confidence historical docs under the agreed archive structure for one release cycle when useful provenance remains.
Delete high-confidence stale docs whose remaining value is already preserved by git history, changelog, releases, task-engine history, or canonical docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Every removed or archived documentation file has evidence showing why the active repo no longer needs it.
High-confidence delete candidates are removed without breaking links, package files, generated docs, or agent routing.
Medium-confidence historical material is clearly marked as archive-only and no longer appears in active navigation.
readyQueue.summary: Apply the inventory decisions to stale planning docs, implemented phase plans, old transcript exports, miscellaneous docs, and duplicate surfaces without losing anything beyond history.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

readyQueue.title: Add documentation governance checks
technicalScope: Add or update checks for root Markdown allowlists, generated doc drift, orphan .ai sources, lifecycle metadata requirements, broken links after moves, and package-files documentation inclusion.
Add a review cadence or shrink target for docs-from-ai exceptions so generated coverage improves over time.
Document the repair flow for failed documentation governance checks in maintainer and agent-appropriate surfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-merge or maintainer gates fail when new root docs, unmapped generated docs, stale agent sources, or missing lifecycle metadata violate policy.
The documentation repair workflow is documented and points to the documentation module commands where applicable.
The final docs organization is protected by automated checks rather than relying on maintainer memory.
readyQueue.summary: Add CI or maintainer checks that keep the human-vs-agent documentation split, root allowlist, generated-doc parity, lifecycle metadata, and stale-doc controls from drifting again.

metadata.sourcePlan: documentation organization and human-vs-agent optimization plan

suggestedNext.title: Define lease UX status contract and concise blocked responses
suggestedNext.approach: Narrow Phase 93 to the foundation that downstream UX work needs: expose a stable lease status vocabulary and make lease-denied command responses compact, truthful, and actionable. Follow-up tickets in the same phase cover extension controls, bounded wait behavior, and suspect drift detection.
technicalScope: Extend lease status/classification helpers so callers with an agentSessionId can distinguish lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, and stale/invalid lease states; reserve suspect/drift semantics for the suspect-drift follow-up ticket.
Revise workspace-edit-lease-held and related non-owner responses to include holder session id, task id when present, expiry, available alternatives, and one recommended next action.
Keep blocked responses compact for agents and avoid suggestions that require broad instruction/catalog reloads or expensive polling.
Document the response/status vocabulary consumed by the extension and follow-up same-phase tickets.
Add focused tests for status classification and denied-response payloads.
acceptanceCriteria: Denied claim, heartbeat, and release paths for active leases held by another session return holder identity, task id when present, expiry, alternatives, and one recommended next action in stable JSON.
A caller-provided agentSessionId can classify lease-free, lease-held-by-me, lease-held-by-other, and stale/invalid states without opening task SQLite or mutating the lease.
Agent-facing blocked copy is concise and does not tell agents to reload broad instructions or poll aggressively.
The shared vocabulary is documented in the relevant command instruction or contract surface for extension consumers.
Automated tests cover same-holder, other-holder, missing, stale/invalid, and compact blocked-response cases.
suggestedNext.summary: Define the shared lease status contract and low-token blocked-response behavior that Phase 93 UX work builds on.
suggestedNext.description: Define the shared lease status contract and low-token blocked-response behavior that Phase 93 UX work builds on.
suggestedNext.risk: Medium: this changes command response contracts that agents and the extension will consume; keep the schema additive and deterministic.

metadata.roadmapSlice: multi-agent workspace lease and task-state authority
goals: Make the safe path feel like guidance instead of a mysterious failure.
Reduce wasted AI credits when an agent cannot edit yet.
Help users recover from stale or suspect lease states without manual file surgery.
blockers: T100188 is completed and provides clone-local lease commands under git common-dir.
Extension work still needs a stable session identifier where available; fallback behavior must be explicit when no session id exists.
The exact edit-capable flows that should honor waitForLease are not enumerated yet; this phase must define the first supported integration points before wiring broad enforcement.
implementationNotes: Use git common-dir for clone-local coordination state; do not store active workspace leases in branch-local task SQLite.
Keep responses compact for agents and avoid broad instruction/catalog reloads in blocked or read-only states.
Prefer fail-safe behavior for file mutation, stash, checkpoint, and task-state authority conflicts.

Current code exposes claim/heartbeat/release/status commands, workspace-coordination-status, a status-bar posture, and a read-only Coordination status card. It does not yet expose held-by-me vs held-by-other status, suspect drift status, extension lease actions, or waitForLease behavior.

ticketReview.verdict: Direction is truthful, but the original ticket was too broad for one implementation task and implied several states/behaviors that are not present yet.
evidence: T100188 is completed with lease command delivery evidence.
src/modules/task-engine/workspace-edit-lease-commands-runtime.ts has claim, heartbeat, release, and workspace-edit-status command behavior.

src/modules/task-engine/coordination/build-workspace-coordination-status.ts emits read-only posture and lease active/stale information, but not holder identity or drift comparison against the lease fingerprint.
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/extension.ts renders only a compact coordination posture in the status bar.
extensions/cursor-workflow-cannon/src/views/status/render-status-tab.ts renders a read-only Coordination card with lease active/stale/none only.
missingInformation: Which VS Code/session identifier should be treated as the durable agentSessionId for held-by-me vs held-by-other classification.
Which commands or edit-capable flows should accept waitForLease in the first implementation slice.
Whether recover stale and claim/release actions should require explicit per-click policy approval copy in the extension or reuse existing command-client approval handling.
How suspect state should be prioritized relative to dirty_workspace, dirty_task_db, and lease_held posture when multiple signals are true.

maintainerDelivery.reminder: GitHub owns branch, PR, review, merge. Branch from the phase integration branch before the first implementation commit; run run-transition start no later than that commit. workspace-kit run-transition owns task lifecycle evidence.

workspacePhaseScheduling.assignPolicy: Leading-digit phaseKeys sort relative to workspace current kit phase; targets strictly before current are rejected. Phase keys without leading digits skip numeric floor comparison.

cae.agentRenderHint: If present, summarize CAE shadow outcome in one short paragraph; when traceId is set, operators can run cae-explain / cae-get-trace.
